title: M5 integration tests
analysis:
This task creates comprehensive end-to-end integration tests exercising multiple M5 features together through the full pipeline (parse → compile → eval/check → verify). All individual M5 features (traits, enums, guards, collections, lambdas, occurrences, connect/chain, ports, user functions, geometry ops) are already implemented across the reify-syntax, reify-compiler, reify-eval, and reify-expr crates.

The existing e2e test pattern (see crates/reify-eval/tests/e2e_bracket.rs, fn_eval.rs, occurrence_eval.rs, connect_eval.rs, guard_eval.rs) uses: (1) parse source via reify_syntax::parse(), (2) compile via reify_compiler::compile(), (3) eval/check via Engine::new(checker, planner).eval/check(), (4) assert on values/constraints.

Key adaptations from the task description to actual implementation:
- No 'Mass' or 'Pressure' dimension types → use Length, Scalar, Real, Int instead
- No 'forall' keyword → use .all(|b| ...) method call on collections
- No 'realize {}' block → geometry is produced via let bindings calling geometry functions (e.g., let body = cylinder(...))
- circular_pattern takes 9 positional args: (target, ox, oy, oz, ax, ay, az, count, angle)
- No 'module' declaration in parser → multi-module test uses filesystem temp dirs with ModuleResolver
- 'structure def' (with 'def') is valid syntax for definitions vs instances

Tests go in crates/reify-eval/tests/m5_integration.rs following the existing convention where integration tests live alongside other eval integration tests. Example .ri files go in examples/.

prerequisites.description: Create examples/m5_trait_structure.ri — structure implementing a trait with conformance checking
Create examples/m5_guarded_enum.ri — guarded declarations with enum matching (where clause + enum variant comparison)
Create examples/m5_connect_chain.ri — occurrence definitions with ports, connect, and chain desugaring
Create examples/m5_user_function.ri — user-defined function called in let binding, result used in constraint
Create examples/m5_geometry.ri — cylinder + circular_pattern geometry through full pipeline

steps.description: Write test: trait_implementing_structure — parse m5_trait_structure.ri, compile, verify no errors, eval, check trait conformance (trait with required param + constraint, structure satisfying it). Assert: parse OK, compile OK (no error diagnostics), eval produces correct param values, constraint from trait is satisfied.
Fix trait_implementing_structure test if needed — adjust .ri syntax or assertions to match actual parser/compiler behavior. Verify test passes.

Write test: multi_module_import — create temp dir with two .ri files (one defining a structure, one importing it), use ModuleResolver + compile_project to compile both modules, verify import resolution succeeds, sub-component from imported structure is compiled correctly.
Fix multi_module_import test if needed — adjust source syntax, resolver paths, or assertions. Verify test passes.

Write test: guarded_enum_declarations — parse m5_guarded_enum.ri, compile, eval with guard true/false. Verify: enum compiles, where-clause with enum comparison creates guarded groups, conditional member activation works, match expression evaluates correctly.
Fix guarded_enum_declarations test if needed — adjust enum/guard/match syntax or assertions. Verify test passes.

Write test: collection_lambda_operations — inline source with list literal, .map(|x| ...) method call, .count property, and .all(|x| ...) quantifier. Parse, compile, eval, verify: list is created, map produces transformed list, count returns correct int, all/any returns correct bool.
Fix collection_lambda_operations test if needed — adjust collection syntax, lambda syntax, or method call names. Verify test passes.

Write test: connect_occurrence_chain — parse m5_connect_chain.ri with occurrence definitions having ports, and a structure containing sub occurrences with chain desugaring. Verify: occurrences compile with EntityKind::Occurrence, chain produces correct number of connections, compatibility constraints are all Satisfied.
Fix connect_occurrence_chain test if needed — adjust port/chain/connect syntax or assertions. Verify test passes.

Write test: user_fn_with_constraint — parse m5_user_function.ri defining a function and a structure that calls it in a let binding, with a constraint on the result. Verify: function compiles, let binding evaluates to correct value, constraint evaluates correctly (satisfied or violated as designed).
Fix user_fn_with_constraint test if needed — adjust function syntax, type annotations, or assertions. Verify test passes.

Write test: geometry_cylinder_pattern — parse m5_geometry.ri with cylinder + circular_pattern through the full build pipeline (with OCCT kernel). Verify: geometry let bindings compile into realization ops, build produces valid STEP output containing ISO-10303-21 header. (Mirror e2e_bracket.rs pattern with real kernel.)
Fix geometry_cylinder_pattern test if needed — adjust geometry function call syntax (positional args for circular_pattern), kernel setup, or assertions. Verify test passes.

Write test: combined_m5_features — a single larger test combining multiple M5 features: enum + trait + guarded declaration + function call + constraint. Parse inline source, compile, eval, check. This exercises feature interaction paths that individual tests may miss.
Fix combined_m5_features test if needed — adjust syntax or assertions for the combined scenario. Verify test passes.

design_decisions.decision: Place integration tests in crates/reify-eval/tests/m5_integration.rs rather than workspace-root tests/
design_decisions.rationale: The workspace has no root-level lib.rs/main.rs, so workspace-level integration tests won't compile. All existing e2e tests (e2e_bracket.rs, fn_eval.rs, etc.) live in crates/reify-eval/tests/ — this follows that established convention.

design_decisions.decision: Adapt task description syntax to match actual parser/compiler capabilities
design_decisions.rationale: The task description uses hypothetical syntax (Mass type, Pressure type, forall keyword, realize block, named-arg geometry calls) that doesn't match the implemented grammar. Tests must use what actually works: Length/Scalar/Real types, .all() method instead of forall, let-binding geometry calls, positional circular_pattern args.

design_decisions.decision: Use MockConstraintChecker for most tests, real SimpleConstraintChecker + OCCT kernel only for geometry test
design_decisions.rationale: Following existing patterns: fn_eval.rs and occurrence_eval.rs use MockConstraintChecker for speed. Only e2e_bracket.rs and connect_eval.rs (incompatible test) use real checkers. Geometry test needs OCCT for real STEP export verification.

design_decisions.decision: Multi-module test uses temp directory with ModuleResolver, not example .ri files
design_decisions.rationale: Multi-module import requires filesystem structure that ModuleResolver can traverse. Using tempdir within the test (following import_resolve_tests.rs pattern) ensures test isolation and avoids coupling example files to internal module paths.

design_decisions.decision: One test file with all scenarios rather than separate files
design_decisions.rationale: All 7+ scenarios share the same dependency set (reify-syntax, reify-compiler, reify-eval, reify-constraints). A single m5_integration.rs file with helper functions is cleaner than 7 separate files, and follows the task description's file organization.

reuse.how: Extract common helper: parse source, assert no parse errors, compile, assert no error diagnostics, eval/check via Engine. Identical to run_bracket_e2e() and the pattern in fn_eval.rs.
Use MockConstraintChecker::new() for tests that don't need real constraint evaluation (default returns Satisfied).
For the geometry test, use the real constraint checker and OCCT kernel following the e2e_bracket pattern.
For multi-module test, create temp dirs with .ri files and use ModuleResolver::new() + compile_project() to test import resolution end-to-end.

reuse.what: Value constructors (mm, meters, etc.) and ID helpers (vcid, cnid)
reuse.how: Use mm(), meters(), vcid() etc. for constructing expected values in assertions.
